Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The study context is salt stress using NaCl treatment.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods were used for this module: Y2H, luciferase complementation, colocalization/localization, and competition assay.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Competition assays used 35S-Empty and 35S-GmF3′H constructs; localization vectors also use 35S-GFP constructs. The paper explicitly mentions 35S constructs.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: AsA was measured in soybean seed coat and leaves.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The study repeatedly refers to 3′-hydroxylated/dihydroxylated flavonoid compounds and anthocyanin/proanthocyanidin-related outputs.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: GmF3′H functions in a branch of flavonoid biosynthesis, but the exact label 'branch point' is an interpretation rather than explicit wording.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper does not specify exact substrates, only that F3′H catalyzes synthesis of 3′-hydroxylated flavonoid compounds/conversion of flavonols into dihydroxylated flavonoids.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is consistent with the paper’s description of F3′H promoting 3′-hydroxylated/dihydroxylated flavonoid compounds.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Direct connection to flavonoid products and indirect promotion of AsA through competition with CSN5B/VTC1 is the central model of the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These correspond to flavonoid and vitamin classes, and AsA is explicitly called vitamin C.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper uses salt stress with 100-150 mM NaCl in soybean and 50-125 mM in Arabidopsis.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in the study for GmF3′H.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state pWMV069-35Spro:GmF3′H-GFP and 35S constructs in assays.
